channel.title: nexusEVnews
channel.description: Independent EV and renewable energy news, curated daily. Electric vehicles, solar, battery tech, and charging infrastructure.

item.title: BYD’s Megawatt Flash Charger Passed an Unscripted Test: A Wrecked Car, 30 Percent, No Setup
item.dc:creator: Out of Spec Reviews
item.description: Out of Spec Reviews flew to Beijing to verify BYD’s flash charging claims on a damaged Denza Z9 GT with no preconditioning. From 30 to 97 percent in just under eight minutes. The hardware draws 500 kW from the grid and supplements it with two 200 kWh on-site battery packs at 1,000 volts. BYD plans 20,000 units in China by end of 2026 and 3,000 in Europe near term.

item.title: This German Factory Stores Summer Heat Underground and Pays Almost Nothing for Energy
item.dc:creator: renewables film
item.description: A metal parts manufacturer in southwest Germany heats and cools its factory for 1.6 cents per kWh with no gas connection. Solar collectors charge 5,000-plus cubic meters of soil beneath the building each summer; heat pumps draw it back through 45 km of pipes in winter. The system paid back in three and a half years.

item.title: The Updated Audi Q4 e-tron Gets the Interior It Always Needed. Charging Speed Is Still the Weak Link.
item.dc:creator: Electrifying
item.description: Electrifying reviews the updated Q4 e-tron: up to 370 miles of range, a new dual-screen interior, vehicle-to-load, and DC charging up to 185 kW on the top model. UK pricing expected from around £41,000. Charging speed still trails several rivals.

item.title: They Installed an £8,000 Home Battery With No Solar. Two Months Later, Here Are the Numbers.
item.dc:creator: iHeat
item.description: iHeat revisits a Juracell battery install at a UK home using 15,000 to 20,000 kWh a year. Daily costs dropped from £10-15 to around £5.50. Payback projected at two and a half to three years. The hybrid inverter supports solar as a future add-on without any equipment replacement.

item.title: Tesla’s Three-Row Model Y L Is Already on Sale in China. We Sat in Every Seat.
item.dc:creator: Kim Java
item.description: Kim Java walks through the Model Y L at Tesla’s Beijing delivery center alongside a Juniper Model Y and a Model X. Seven inches longer, six-seat captain layout, 89 cu ft of cargo. Third-row headroom impressed at 5’10”. US prototype spotted; estimated price $55,000 to $59,000 if it arrives.

item.title: All Six World Car Awards Went to EVs. The 2026 NY Auto Show Made the Case Without Apology.
item.dc:creator: Forbes
item.description: The 2026 New York Auto Show put electric vehicles in nearly every exhibit, from the Hyundai Ioniq 6N winning World Performance Car of the Year to Lucid's Gravity claiming the World Luxury Car title with 820 horsepower and 450 miles of range. The Porsche Cayenne Electric made its North American debut, redesigned from the ground up. And when the World Car Awards tallied all six categories, every winner was electric. Forbes walked the full floor.

item.title: Greg Jackson Would Fix the UK Energy Market in Three Steps. The First One Is Hiding in Plain Sight.
item.dc:creator: Everything Electric Show
item.description: Octopus Energy CEO Greg Jackson, behavioral scientist Rory Sutherland, and Robert Llewellyn on a live Oxford Street panel. UK electricity costs four times more than gas; in Norway it is 1.15 times. Jackson's three fixes: zonal pricing, more onshore wind, and long-distance interconnectors. Sutherland covers EV adoption psychology and the sigmoid curve. Llewellyn adds that 100 million-plus EVs have already displaced the equivalent of 70 percent of Iran's total fossil fuel output annually.

item.title: China's Electric Truck Market Crossed 50 Percent of Monthly Sales. The IEA Called It.
item.dc:creator: Just Have a Think
item.description: Per the IEA's Global EV Outlook 2025, electric heavy trucks reached around 22 percent of new truck sales in China in H1 2025 and reportedly crossed 50 percent of monthly sales in December 2025. More than 80 percent of global electric truck sales happen in China. This video covers the economics, CATL's battery swapping network covering 150,000 km of expressways, and how Europe and the US compare.

item.title: Rivian R2, Volvo EX60, Tesla Cybercab, and Kia EV3 All Entered Production in the Same Week
item.dc:creator: Out of Spec Bits
item.description: A tornado hit Rivian's Normal, Illinois factory and production of sellable R2 units started anyway. The Volvo EX60 entered production in Sweden with European deliveries this summer. The Kia EV3 entered pre-production in Mexico at an expected starting price around $35,000. The Tesla Cybercab entered production as well. Also covered: Hyundai Ioniq 3 reveal, Porsche Cayenne Coupe EV, Tesla Model Y L US testing, and the electric-only Mercedes C-Class.

item.title: The Alpine A390 GT Is Fast Enough and Comfortable Enough to Make You Forget It's Supposed to Be Sporty
item.dc:creator: AutoTopNL
item.description: Three motors, 400 horsepower, 661 Nm, 89 kWh battery, WLTP range of 557 km. AutoTopNL tests the Alpine A390 GT on the Autobahn and records a 4.73-second 0-100 km/h run. The car weighs over 2,100 kg and handles it with hydraulic bump stops and a compliant suspension. A GTS variant with 470 horsepower and 0-100 in 3.9 seconds arrives later in 2026.

item.title: From Battery Startup to World’s Biggest EV Maker: Inside BYD’s Five-Year Run
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Television
item.description: In five years China moved from sixth in global auto exports to first. BYD beat Tesla in total EV sales in 2025, employs close to a million people, and files 52 patents every working day. Bloomberg reports from Paris, where BYD launched a luxury EV and where Ford CEO Jim Farley described seeing BYD's operation as the most humbling experience of his career. A 100% US tariff is keeping the cars out of American dealerships. Trade talks between Washington and Beijing are underway.

item.title: GM Hits Pause on EV Trucks. Mercedes Goes Electric-Only on the C-Class. A Gas Car Podcast Takes Stock.
item.dc:creator: This Car Pod
item.description: GM has announced it is pausing its next generation of full-size electric trucks. The Ford Lightning was already cancelled. Meanwhile Mercedes revealed the new C-Class as electric-only, going on sale in early 2027. The hosts of a gas car podcast picture themselves in a nursing home years from now, laughing at the idea that soccer moms once drove children to practice in cars powered by controlled combustion. Their punchline: that moment hasn't arrived in America yet. European EV sales in March rose 50%.

item.title: Formula E's Gen 4 Car Does 0-60 in 1.8 Seconds on Road Tires. The Sport Is Not Playing Around Anymore.
item.dc:creator: Aldas
item.description: Formula E's Gen 4 car produces 600 kW, roughly 805 horsepower, and reaches 0-60 mph in 1.88 seconds on grooved road-spec tires. It laps permanent tracks around 10 seconds faster than the Gen 3 EVO. The series is shifting toward more permanent circuits to match the new performance level. Development driver James Rossa, a former F1 test driver at BAR and Force India, says Gen 4 now offers Formula 2 and 3 drivers a genuine world championship alternative to Formula 1.

item.title: Americans Can't Buy Chinese EVs. They Can't Stop Watching Videos About Them.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Television
item.description: A 100% tariff plus a 2025 tech-linked ban keep Chinese EVs out of US showrooms. The average new US car price is around $50,000. Multiple capable Chinese EVs are priced well under $40,000 at home. Marques Brownlee's review of the Xiaomi SU7 passed 10 million views. Bloomberg looks at the technology gap, the social media appetite, and why the future of the US auto industry may hinge on how open American companies become to Chinese technology.

item.title: Three Things Volkswagen Needs to Fix Before Its Chinese Rivals Close the Gap Completely
item.dc:creator: Out of Spec Roaming
item.description: Out of Spec Roaming drives the VW ID.7 Tourer GTX over a Norwegian mountain pass in winter. The car averaged 19.2 kWh per 100 km over 478 km of cold driving and charged from 17% to 53% in 11 minutes at close to 200 kW. Three things still need fixing: software, interior quality, and charging speed. A Rivian/VW joint venture is working on future architecture. The urgency: Chinese EV brands are already offering better interiors and more technology at comparable prices.

item.title: Hyundai's Ioniq V Debuts in Beijing With a 27-Inch 4K Screen and 600 km of Range
item.dc:creator: OtoFootage / Car and Driver
item.description: The Hyundai Ioniq V is the first dedicated production model for Hyundai's revived Ioniq brand in China, debuting at the 2026 Beijing Auto Show. Built on an 800-volt eGMP platform and derived from the Venus concept, it measures just under 193 inches long on a 2.9-meter wheelbase and is confirmed for over 600 km of range on the CLTC standard. A 27-inch ultra-thin 4K panoramic display spans the dashboard. The interior adds Airhug massage seats, Nebula ambient lighting, Dolby Atmos sound, and rear-wheel-drive and all-wheel-drive powertrain options. Hyundai plans to supply Britain, Europe, and the Middle East from China after a late-2026 production start.

item.title: Tesla's Longer Model Y Has a Functional Third Row and a Strong Case for Family Buyers
item.dc:creator: Out of Spec Reviews
item.description: Out of Spec Reviews gets a first look at the Tesla Model Y L in China. The L stretches the standard platform to fit a real third row: at 6'1", Kyle sat in all three rows simultaneously with knee clearance in the back. The top-spec long-range variant carries approximately 84-to-88 kWh of LG cells, dual-motor all-wheel drive, and 250 kW peak DC charging. Second-row seating is captain's chairs only. Adaptive damping and double glazing are standard. The wireless phone charger has been upgraded to 50 watts with active cooling. A US prototype has been spotted.

item.title: A $35,000 Manual Sports Car vs a $55,000 Electric Sedan: Which One Do You Actually Want to Drive?
item.dc:creator: Gjeebs
item.description: Gjeebs drives the Subaru BRZ TS and Tesla Model 3 Performance back to back. BRZ TS: 228 hp, 187 lb-ft, around 2,900 pounds, six-speed manual only, Brembo brakes, Michelin Pilot Sport 4 tires, roughly $35,000. Model 3 Performance: 510 hp, 553 lb-ft, 2.9 seconds to 60, about 300 miles of range, around $55,000, and roughly 1,100 pounds heavier. The BRZ TS is naturally aspirated with no direct competition, good for track days and learning the limits of a car. The Model 3 wins on every daily-use metric. Both are valid at different life stages.

item.title: BMW Brings Three Neue Klasse Premieres to Beijing, Including an i3 With 1,000 km of Range
item.dc:creator: BMW Group
item.description: BMW revealed the iX3 Long Wheelbase, i3 Long Wheelbase, and refreshed 7 Series at Auto China 2026 in Beijing, all built on the Neue Klasse platform. The i3 Long Wheelbase, the second-generation electric 3 Series for China, is rated at over 1,000 km on CLTC. The i7 electric 7 Series carries close to 800 km of CLTC range. A fourth car, the iX3 Flow Edition, uses E-Ink panels on major exterior body components. All vehicles share BMW's panoramic iDrive and Level 2 navigation-guided assistance developed with Chinese AI partner Momenta. BMW has committed to 40 new or updated models by 2027.

item.title: The Ioniq 5 N and the Dodge Charger EV Both Claim to Be Fun. One of Them Actually Is.
item.dc:creator: The Drive
item.description: Kyle and Nick from The Drive take the Hyundai Ioniq 5 N and Dodge Charger Daytona EV to Chuck Walla Raceway and a canyon road. Both cars have over 640 horsepower and all-wheel drive. The Charger weighs approximately 5,800 pounds; the Ioniq 5 N is roughly 1,000 pounds lighter. On track, the Ioniq 5 N was communicative with paddle-operated simulated shifts and adjustable torque vectoring. The Charger showed throttle lag and persistent understeer. Porsche has since begun adding virtual gear shifts to its own EVs after evaluating the Ioniq 5 N.
